diagrams.title: C++ Data Types Classification
diagrams.description: A hierarchical tree diagram showing the classification of data types in C++: Fundamental types (int, float, double, char, bool, void) at the top, branching into Derived types (array, pointer, reference, function) and User-defined types (class, struct, union, enum). This helps learners understand the type system organization.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the organization and hierarchy of C++ data types, helping learners recognize which category different data types belong to

diagrams.title: Variable Declaration Structure
diagrams.description: A visual representation showing the anatomy of a C++ variable declaration, with a type box on the left connected to a variable name box, and optionally a value box. Includes memory representation boxes showing how different types occupy different amounts of memory (int=4 bytes, double=8 bytes, char=1 byte).
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the syntax and components of variable declaration in C++, along with memory allocation for different data types

diagrams.title: C++ Operators Categories
diagrams.description: A categorized grid layout showing the main operator types in C++ with visual icons: Arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /, %), Assignment operators (=, +=, -=, *=, /=), Comparison/Relational operators (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=), Logical operators (&&, ||, !), and Increment/Decrement operators (++, --). Each category in a colored box with example expressions.
diagrams.purpose: Provides a quick visual reference for all operator types in C++ with their symbols and example usage
